text.title: structured-tool-errors Procedure
text.summary: Six-step migration from free-form errors to the structured envelope + runner dispatch.

step.name: inventory-error-paths
step.action: Walk every `raise` / `return error` path. For each, record: trigger condition, current message, upstream HTTP status, whether the failure is transient.
step.output: Table `(path, trigger, transient_bool)` for every error path.

step.name: assign-semantic-codes
step.action: Translate each upstream status to a semantic UPPER_SNAKE code (`429 â†’ UPSTREAM_RATE_LIMITED`, `401 â†’ AUTH_REQUIRED`, etc.). Reuse codes across paths where the failure category is the same.

step.name: assign-recovery-hint
step.action: Apply the decision tree: rate-limit/transient â†’ `RETRY_LATER`; bad-args â†’ `CHECK_INPUT`; auth â†’ `NEEDS_AUTH`; unrecoverable â†’ `REPORT_TO_USER`; alternative-exists â†’ `TRY_ALTERNATIVE`.
step.decision-gate: If the path is genuinely unrecoverable, do NOT assign `RETRY_LATER` "to be safe" â€” that just burns the retry budget.

step.name: emit-envelope
step.action: Rewrite the error response to emit the envelope: `{error: {code, message â‰¤240 chars, recoveryHint, traceId: ulid(), retry_after_seconds?, details: sanitised}}`. Strip stack frames; preserve only what the agent can act on.

step.name: wire-runner-dispatch
step.action: Add the hintâ†’action map: `RETRY_LATER` â†’ wait clamped(retry_after_seconds, 1, 300); `CHECK_INPUT` â†’ return to model; `TRY_ALTERNATIVE` â†’ return + signal; `REPORT_TO_USER` â†’ halt; `NEEDS_AUTH` â†’ halt + request creds. Add per-tool retry budget cap.
step.output: Runner dispatcher executing the closed map.

step.name: validate-and-smoke
step.action: Run `scripts/validate-structured-tool-errors.py sample.json` against each sample error body produced by the tool. Add a runtime smoke test in the tool's test suite that asserts every error path emits a valid envelope.
step.decision-gate: If any path emits an invalid envelope â€” block the deploy.
